primary.justification: The chapter's entire architecture is the systematic explanation of weather — its five elements (temperature, precipitation, atmospheric pressure, wind, humidity), the instruments that measure each, and how meteorologists predict it. Weather is explicitly named as a 'key natural phenomenon' in C-6.1. Remove this competency and the chapter dissolves entirely. The chapter title directly names weather and weather instruments as its sole subject.

The Weather Instruments section on Temperature explicitly addresses calculation of temperature range (max − min) and mean daily temperature — this requires students to represent and analyse numerical data. This is one named subsection within the larger chapter about weather elements and measurement, qualifying as present but not substantive.
